primary_job: Create a thoughtful, low-value, non-conditional relationship gesture that supports the decision process without pressuring or bribing the customer.
best_for: After an in-person meeting as a thank-you or useful follow-up
When the gift is directly useful to the home-energy decision
Longer-cycle relationship nurturing for high-trust local installers
A small educational or practical item paired with a recap
Customer has engaged respectfully but needs time
bad_for: As a response to price objection
When customer is silent and may feel pressured
When it could look like a bribe
When the installer cannot explain why it helps the customer
timing_guidance: Use only after a meaningful interaction, never as the first follow-up.
Use after a site visit when paired with a useful recap.
Do not use while an unresolved price objection is active.
Keep value low and clearly non-conditional.

safe_gift_examples.name: Printed personalized solar roadmap
safe_gift_examples.why_it_fits: It is a decision-support artifact rather than a perk.

safe_gift_examples.name: Home energy checklist pack
safe_gift_examples.why_it_fits: It helps the household prepare documents and questions.

safe_gift_examples.name: Low-value local coffee card after site visit
safe_gift_examples.why_it_fits: It is a thank-you gesture, not tied to signing.

safe_gift_examples.name: Printed roof/installation photo summary
safe_gift_examples.why_it_fits: It documents the site-specific visit and supports stakeholder review.

unsafe_gift_examples: Gift framed as available only if they sign soon
Gift that distracts from an unresolved price or trust objection
Gift that implies special treatment not offered transparently
output_artifacts: Reasoning for why it is appropriate
tone_guidance: Tie it to usefulness, not persuasion.

message_examples.message: Thank you for taking the time today. I included a short printed summary of what we checked and the two remaining decisions so you can review it calmly.
Here is a small decision pack with the key assumptions and questions for your review at home. No rush from our side; it is meant to make the comparison easier.

success_indicators: Customer acknowledges the recap or decision artifact
Customer shares it with another stakeholder
Customer asks a clearer follow-up question
debrief_questions: Did it create or clarify a next step?
Should future gifts be avoided for this customer?
guardrails: Do not use gifts as pressure.
Do not use gifts to overcome price objections.
Do not represent a gift as proof of product value.
